gauge.name: Red River at Index

impacts.statement: Record flooding of tens of thousands of acres will take place.
Expect widespread inundation of thousand of acres of farmland between and near levees.
A 24 hour flood watch will commence at the Index bridge on U.S. highway 71.  The bridge is in danger of being overtopped.
General inundation of farm and grazing lowlands will occur.  Ranchers should have completed evacuation of cattle and equipment to higher ground.  Expect water to creep well up on levee systems.
U.S. Highway 71 Red River bridge closed.

General inundation of farm and grazing lowlands will occur.  Ranchers should have completed evacuation of cattle and equipment to higher ground.  U.S. Highway 71 approaches overflow next to the weigh station with the bridge very likely closed.
Water begins to overflow the approaches next to the weigh station at U.S. Highway 71 with the bridge threatened with closure.

Water begins to overflow the approaches next to the weigh station at U.S. Highway 71 with the bridge threatened with closure.  Also high water overflows the north approches to the bridge on the southbound lanes.
Considerable flooding of lowland farm and grazing land will take place.  Ranchers should move cattle and equipment to higher ground.
The Red River is at bankfull with some minor flooding of low pastures. Ranchers are advised to move cattle to higher ground.
Considerable backwater flooding will take place on Walnut Bayou at its confluence with the Red River to near Tom, Oklahoma.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey
Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
